mechanism_narrative: A steady rise in real GDP signals expanding aggregate demand, which lifts corporate revenues and earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The 60-day forward return Rank IC of year-over-year GDP growth is below 0.03 or the signal fails to predict positive returns in >55% of expansion quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in monthly payroll gains below 150k signals labor market weakness that reduces aggregate income and consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: More than 40% of triggered instances yield positive 20-day SPX returns, or the mean return in triggered vs. non-triggered periods is not statistically negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is low relative to rising consumer prices, real interest rates become negative, discouraging saving and encouraging equity investment as a hedge, boosting S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The average 20-day SPX return in triggered quarters is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) or the joint signal produces a Spearman rank IC below 0.03 on a 2-year rolling basis.

mechanism_narrative: Falling unemployment during the early recovery phase of the business cycle signals improving labor market conditions that boost consumer confidence and spending, lifting S&P 500 returns over the near term.
failure_condition: The signal does not produce positive average forward returns in NBER-defined recession trough periods, or the walk-forward long-short spread from UNRATE direction is below 2% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in retail sales reflects strong consumer demand, which directly supports corporate revenues and positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over the next trading week.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward Rank IC of RSAFS month-over-month growth remains below 0.03 for any consecutive 2-year period, or the strategy loses money in expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in industrial production indicates slowing manufacturing activity, which precedes broader economic weakness and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: More than 50% of triggered instances produce positive SPX returns in the following 20 trading days, or the mean return in triggered windows is not significantly negative (one-sided p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate undershoots a simple Taylor rule benchmark (inflation plus 1% real rate), monetary policy is overly accommodative and compresses risk premiums, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The average 20-day SPX return in triggered periods is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the rule’s Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts during a monetary easing cycle confirm that lower rates are stimulating residential investment, a powerful leading indicator that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The 60-day forward Rank IC of HOUST year-over-year growth is below 0.03 in easing periods (FEDFUNDS declining > 50bps over 6 months), or the signal fails to produce positive returns in >60% of such regimes.
